dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (kah on berapa)
notes: Formal kah on 'berapa' (how much/many). The '-nya' suffix on 'harga' is a definite-article-like marker ('the price'). VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'How much does it cost?' is more conversational English; this fixture keeps the literal 'price' since the source uses the noun explicitly.
